nodes.label: Token-Array Architecture
nodes.rationale: Multi-stage pipeline designed for modularity; output of one stage becomes input of next. Replaced marker-based gsub monolith with token-array approach for better error isolation and incremental improvement.

nodes.label: Lexical Override Tables (Vowel Quality)
nodes.rationale: Blanket phonological rules for vowel quality regress (e.g. stressed ɛ→i at -164, word-final ʊ→ɛ at -11). Vowel quality is lexical not phonological in Irish; each fix requires word-by-word exception tables rather than context-free rules.

nodes.label: Connacht-First Strategy
nodes.rationale: Design B (pass-per-module) chosen specifically to enable Connacht-first development, with architecture allowing later dialect extension to Munster and Ulster.

nodes.label: Stress Placement Before Reduction Ordering
nodes.rationale: Stress-moved-up ordering chosen so stress assignment occurs before vowel reduction, enabling reduction pass to be conditioned on stress position.

nodes.label: Epenthesis Conditioned on Short Stressed Vowels
nodes.rationale: Epenthesis conditioned on short stressed vowels rather than monosyllables to improve accuracy on multi-word phrases and non-initial syllables.
nodes.norm_label: epenthesis conditioned on short stressed vowels

nodes.label: Disabled Sandhi Processing
nodes.rationale: Sandhi rules (word-boundary phonetic changes) are disabled. Each word processes in isolation, which causes inaccuracies in fluent text. Multi-word fix attempted in pass 14 but limited in scope.

nodes.label: Marker-Based to Token-Array Refactor
nodes.rationale: Original monolith used marker-based transformations (bh -> MKR_BH -> resolution). Refactored to token-array approach where each token carries orthography + phoneme + feature metadata. Reason: better error isolation, incremental improvement, modular testing.
